name: RENAL MASS
definition: attenuation higher than normal renal tissue mimicking a renal mass persistence of normal septal cortex / excessive infolding of cortex usually in the sonographic mass of reniform appearance with a central hyperechoic region surrounded by
dominant_finding: poorly marginated area of diminished enhancement
discriminating_features: calcifications generally nonperipheral, sometimes along fibrous capsule

general: calcifications generally nonperipheral, sometimes along fibrous capsule
poorly defined interface between normal renal parenchyma and lesion
enlarged kidney with preservation of reniform shape
CT: poorly marginated area of diminished enhancement
encasement of collecting system without displacement
US: triangular contour + elongation of middle calyx
enhancement pattern like cortex with medulla
hypertrophy usually evident within 2 months; less likely to occur > age 50
